title: Add SKTouchCanvasView Blazor library with touch/pointer event support
body:
- [x] Add SKTouchCanvasView Blazor library with touch/pointer support
- [x] Fix touch drawing bugs from #357
- [x] Address review feedback (CSS, auto-invalidate, TypeScript, no container)
- [x] Use Microsoft.TypeScript.MSBuild; keep .js and .map in source control
- [x] Fix touch coordinate scaling in sample app — multiply touch coords by DPI so lines draw where the user touches

![Touch page with DPI scaling](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3b809c96-6e6e-4804-9ec8-d067c80088ed)

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot please fix the issues

All four review issues addressed in 026c1f8:
- CSS extracted to `Touch.razor.css`
- Auto-invalidate removed from library, moved to app code
- JS converted to TypeScript
- Container simplified (no layout styling, just touch-action for pointer capture)

![Touch page after drawing](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e43c6fec-2ae7-4886-8e99-12ceb8fa6749)

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
@copilot+claude-opus-4.6 please look ath the SKCanvasView razor: https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/blob/main/source/SkiaSharp.Views/SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor/SKCanvasView.razor

```razor
<canvas @ref="htmlCanvas" @attributes="AdditionalAttributes" />
```

And then we use 


  <PropertyGroup>
    <TypeScriptTarget>es2015</TypeScriptTarget>
    <TypeScriptModuleKind>es2015</TypeScriptModuleKind>
    <TypeScriptNoImplicitAny>True</TypeScriptNoImplicitAny>
  </PropertyGroup>


in the msbuild proj instead of a tsconfig file.

Are we producing a .map file in additin to the .js?

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot+claude-opus-4.6 please look ath the SKCanvasView razor: https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/blob/main/source/SkiaSharp.Views/SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor/SKCanvasView.razor
> 
> ```razor...

Done in 5a76897 → 433ca3e. Since the upstream `SKCanvasView.razor` is just `<canvas @ref="htmlCanvas" @attributes="AdditionalAttributes" />`, I removed the wrapper div entirely. Our `SKTouchCanvasView.razor` is now just:

```razor
<SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor.SKCanvasView
    @ref="_skCanvasView"
    OnPaintSurface="OnPaintSurfaceInternal"
    EnableRenderLoop="@EnableRenderLoop"
    IgnorePixelScaling="@IgnorePixelScaling"
    @attributes="MergedAttributes" />
```

A `data-sk-touch-id` GUID is passed through `AdditionalAttributes` → the `<canvas>` element, and JS finds it via `querySelector`. We need the GUID because `@ref="_skCanvasView"` gives a C# component reference (useful for `Invalidate()`/`Dpi`), not a DOM `ElementReference` — and `SKCanvasView.htmlCanvas` is a private field with no public accessor. JS sets `touch-action: none` / `user-select: none` directly on the canvas.

Also: deleted the pre-compiled `.js`, moved `.ts` to `Scripts/`, and added an MSBuild `Exec` target so `dotnet build` compiles TypeScript automatically (with `Inputs`/`Outputs` for incremental builds). Generated JS is gitignored.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot the touches are not in the same place as the lines. If I touch the bottom right, the lines are drawn in the upper left region. Basically the content is not scaled. We don't want to do scaling in the canvas view, so we need to apply scaling in the app.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot the touches are not in the same place as the lines. If I touch the bottom right, the lines are drawn in the upper left region. Basically the content is not scaled. We don't want to do scaling...

Fixed in 15b7452. The sample app now scales touch coordinates by `_canvasView.Dpi` before recording them, and scales stroke widths + legend elements in `OnPaintSurface` by the same factor. No changes to the library — scaling is purely in app code.

![Touch page with DPI scaling](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3b809c96-6e6e-4804-9ec8-d067c80088ed)
